description_long: This is a barrel-shaped stone block with a rounded top and a rectangular base, crafted from a light-colored stone. The front face features a rectangular panel with engraved text, suggesting historical or commemorative significance. The sides and back are smooth, with subtle horizontal grooves encircling the curved surface, mimicking the appearance of a barrel. The ends of the block are rounded and display a simple, circular motif. The stone surface appears weathered, with some roughness and minor chipping along the base. This object is substantial and solid, likely used as a barrier or marker in an urban or historical setting.
description: A barrel-shaped, engraved stone block with a rounded top and rectangular base, featuring weathered light stone and subtle decorative grooves.
description_view_0: The front view shows the engraved panel with text on the curved face, flanked by horizontal grooves and resting on a rectangular base.
description_view_1: The left end view reveals the rounded end with a circular motif and the thickness of the base.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the smooth, unadorned curved surface with horizontal grooves and some chipping at the base.
description_view_3: The right end view mirrors the left, showing the rounded end with a circular motif and the base's profile.
attribute_location_context: historic urban plaza or commemorative park
attribute_installation_method: set on a prepared stone or concrete foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: Minor chipping and surface roughness, with some erosion visible along the base edges.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Engraved text panel on the front face.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated, likely used as a standalone marker or barrier
